draft_hook.text: Hi Maya – noticed Lattice Forge just shipped call summarization for sales teams as your first GA AI feature and are already working on the next two. Given that you're leading the product org through this AI-native shift at a Series B company of ~120 people, and that you're 'always interested in talking to teams thinking about AI feature adoption,' I'd love to share how we're helping product leaders at similar-stage companies accelerate and de-risk exactly that roadmap.
claims_used: Lattice Forge has shipped a GA AI feature: call summarization for sales teams.
Two additional AI features are currently in active development.
Lattice Forge is a Series B company.
Maya explicitly expressed interest in discussing AI feature adoption.
Maya Chen holds the VP of Product title at Lattice Forge.

output.reasoning: Maya Chen is an exact role match (VP of Product — one of the explicitly listed ICP target roles). Lattice Forge is Series B (within ICP stage range), ~120 employees (within 20–250 headcount band), and a B2B SaaS company with a shipped GA AI feature and two more in active development — a textbook product-shape match. The one mild uncertainty is ARR: the $28M figure cited is Series B funding raised, not ARR. However, a Series B backed by Sequoia and Bessemer with enterprise logos like Datadog, Notion, and Atlassian makes it very plausible ARR falls within the $2M–$50M ICP band; arr_match is scored at 0.65 to reflect the absence of an explicit ARR figure rather than a confirmed miss. All claims are grounded with verbatim source quotes. Holistic fit score is 0.88, exceeding the 0.80 auto_add threshold with every claim grounded, so action is auto_add.
